steps.title: Find the slope of line s
steps.content:
Rewrite line $s$ in slope-intercept form:
$$y + 13 = 5x \implies y = 5x - 13$$
Slope of line $s$ = 5, y-intercept = $-13$.

steps.title: Apply the parallel-lines condition
steps.content:
Parallel lines have equal slopes and different y-intercepts. So the slope of line $t$ must equal 5. Test each choice as a possible slope of line $t$:
  • "$-13$": This is the y-intercept of line $s$, not its slope. Slope $-13 eq 5$. ✗
  • "$-5$": Slope $-5 eq 5$. A line with slope $-5$ would be perpendicular in sign but not parallel. ✗
  • "$5$": Slope $5 = 5$. Matches the slope of line $s$. ✓
  • "$13$": Slope $13 eq 5$. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: The slope of any line parallel to $y = 5x - 13$ is 5. That matches the correct answer, so C is correct.
